page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server designed for workloads requiring parallel processing power and high-speed memory. It features 12 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration consists of 12 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, utilizing a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 10.67 GB of memory per core. For acceleration, it integrates two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined total of 96 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression/decompression tasks (gzip and bzip3) all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core CPU performance, including Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory performance is robust, with large-block read and write memory bandwidth scoring in the top 25%. For AI workloads, the dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs enable strong performance in LLM inference, particularly in text generation for small models (top 10%) and medium models (top 25%), while prompt processing and large model inference remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a specialized hardware profile that balances high-end GPU acceleration with a moderate CPU and memory footprint, making it a resource-dense option for GPU-bound tasks without the overhead of excessive CPU cores. The absence of local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. This server is highly suited for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science, image processing, and moderate-scale 3D rendering or ray tracing.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB of total VRAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth. While multi-core CPU metrics are average, the dual Ada Lovelace GPUs provide excellent acceleration for parallel processing. This server does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions. It is highly efficient for GPU-bound workloads, offering a dense accelerator-to-CPU ratio. The configuration is optimized for machine learning inference, image processing, and data science tasks.

tagline: Accelerate machine learning and parallel computing workloads with dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs and high-performance AMD EPYC processing.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB of combined VRAM
12 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processor
128.0 GB of system memory (10.67 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and text generation performance
No local storage included, requiring network storage
Best suited for LLM inference, image processing, and data science
